results.title: Gauging the Gap: Student Perceptions of Skill Proficiency in Skill-Based Education within Schools of Punjab, Pakistan
results.citation: Muhammad Rafiq-uz-Zaman et al. (2025) Gauging the Gap: Student Perceptions of Skill Proficiency in Skill-Based Education within Schools of Punjab, Pakistan doi:10.63056/acad.004.03.0553
results.venue: ACADEMIA International Journal for Social Sciences
results.abstract: This study investigates student-perceived proficiency in soft and practical skills acquired through skill-based education (SBE) in schools. A quantitative approach was employed, utilizing a structured questionnaire administered to 384 twelfth grade students (158 males and 226 females) across 50% public higher secondary schools of three other than divisional headquarters cities of Punjab province, namely Jehlum, Chiniot, and Bahawalnagar. The survey assessed students’ self-reported proficiency in 20 soft skills (e.g., data analysis, communication, critical thinking) and 20 practical skills (e.g., welding, IT skills, cooking), using a five-point Likert scale. Data analysis, involving frequency distributions, weighted scores, mean values, standard deviations, and rank ordering, revealed significant disparities in perceived proficiency. Students reported the lowest levels of proficiency in foundational areas such as data analysis, research skills and critical thinking skills among soft skills, and welding and woodwork among practical skills. While skills such as cultural sensitivity, self-awareness, and sports were rated higher, most skills were only perceived as moderately proficient. From all over soft and practical skills, mostly were slightly proficient.  These findings underscore a significant gap between the intended outcomes of SBE and students’ perceived skill acquisition. The study highlights the need for a reevaluation and refinement of SBE implementation strategies. This research contributes valuable student-centered insights for educators and policymakers seeking to enhance SBE programs in schools. The study's limitations include its reliance on self-reported data, cross-sectional design and limited geographic scope.

results.title: Digital Transformation in Project Management: A Systematic Review and Research Agenda
results.citation: Meiying Chen et al. (2025) Digital Transformation in Project Management: A Systematic Review and Research Agenda doi:10.3390/systems13080625
results.abstract: Digital transformation (DT) fundamentally reshapes how organisations operate, create value, and respond to complex environments through the integration of digital technologies. Beyond mere technical deployment, DT involves behavioural, strategic, and institutional changes. As these transformations are increasingly managed through projects and programmes, project management plays a pivotal role, not only in delivering these transformations but also in enabling them. However, the relationship between DT and project management remains fragmented and insufficiently explored. This paper addresses this gap by systematically reviewing 66 peer-reviewed articles using a qualitative thematic coding approach informed by sociotechnical systems theory. The analysis reveals four interrelated themes: methodologies and the sociotechnical integration of digital tools, misalignments in sociotechnical interfaces, governance and leadership, and industry- or project-specific transformation trajectories. Based on these findings, the paper proposes three key future research agenda: (1) embedding digital tools through methodological mediation and governance integration, (2) governance and leadership as strategic enablers, and (3) advancing sector-specific insights into DT. By offering a structured synthesis and a theory-driven research agenda, this review contributes to a more integrated understanding of how DT unfolds within project-based contexts and lays the groundwork for future interdisciplinary research.

results.title: Between Adoption and Ambiguity: Navigating the AI Policy Vacuum in Pakistani Higher Education
results.citation: Muhammad Rafiq-uz-Zaman (2025) Between Adoption and Ambiguity: Navigating the AI Policy Vacuum in Pakistani Higher Education doi:10.71317/rjsa.003.06.0523
results.abstract: Large language models, grammar assistants, automated analytics, and other artificial intelligence (AI) tools have quickly changed higher education around the globe. However, in Pakistan, their application in an institutional setting has not been tightly regulated. This critical analysis explores the widening divide between individual implementation and official government regulation of AI in Pakistani institutions of higher learning, by referencing the Islamia University of Bahawalpur (IUB) as an allegorical example of policy laggardness in the face of excitement. The article presents arguments based on recent international and open-access literature, such as UNESCO (2023), Jisc (2024), as well as 2024-2025 empirical studies that argue that AI avoidance or silence is worse than directed integration. Even though some universities have developed explicit ethical guidelines on the use of AI, including Oxford, Monash, Stanford, and the National University of Singapore, Higher Education Commission of Pakistan and large universities still use old policies on plagiarism. The paper supports specific changes: the development of a national algorithm of ethical use of AI in the educational process, the creation of university-level policy units in the field of AI, training of faculty employees in pedagogy, reforming the quality assurance standards, and the mandatory publicity of AI employment in academic activities. It presents a case that Pakistan must shift towards rhetorical adoption to responsible integration. As long as organizations such as IUB connect their AI ambitions to transparent governance, Pakistan can be a regional leader of ethical and inclusive AI use in higher education in the Global South.

results.title: Interplay between leadership style and innovative work behavior: mediated by innovation-oriented people management
results.citation: Qiang Fu et al. (2025) Interplay between leadership style and innovative work behavior: mediated by innovation-oriented people management doi:10.1108/ijis-03-2025-0104
results.abstract:
This study aims to investigate how transformational leadership (TL) and innovation-oriented leadership (IOL) influence innovative work behavior (IWB), with innovation-oriented people management (IOPM) serving as a mediating mechanism. It offers a novel dual-path framework to understand the structural and psychological dynamics through which leadership drives innovation.
 
 
 
 A quantitative cross-sectional survey was conducted among 158 employees across innovation-active organizations in Singapore, including sectors such as ICT, manufacturing, construction and retail. Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) and Process Macro (Model 4) with bootstrapping were employed to analyze both direct and indirect effects.
 
 
 
 The dual-path model offers superior predictive power, because both TL and IOL significantly enhance IWB, but through distinct mechanisms. TL operates more autonomously via intrinsic motivation, whereas IOL relies heavily on structured human resource (HR) systems (IOPM). IOPM partially mediates the relationship between both leadership styles and IWB, with a stronger mediating effect observed in the IOL–IWB pathway (51.94%) compared to TL–IWB (26.42%).
 
 
 
 The study is limited by its cross-sectional design, reliance on self-reported data and geographic focus on Singapore. Future research should apply longitudinal, multi-source designs and include additional leadership styles and control variables to improve generalizability.
 
 
 
 Organizations should align leadership strategies with their structural contexts. TL may be more effective in hierarchical or resource-constrained settings, whereas IOL requires supportive HR infrastructures. Policymakers and HR leaders can use these insights to tailor leadership development and innovation-support systems by sector.
 
 
 
 This study highlights the critical role of leadership–HR alignment in shaping workplace innovation and employee empowerment. By clarifying how transformational and innovation-oriented leadership styles foster innovative work behavior, it contributes to building inclusive, participatory and psychologically safe work environments. The findings support broader social objectives of employee well-being, equity in idea contribution and sustainable organizational learning. Moreover, the emphasis on innovation-oriented people management systems offers pathways to enhance workforce adaptability and engagement in the digital age. These insights can inform social policies aimed at fostering human capital development and inclusive innovation across sectors and demographic groups.
 
 
 
 This study introduces an integrated dual-pathway model that distinguishes how transformational and innovation-oriented leadership uniquely influence IWB through IOPM. IOPM is conceptualized as a synthesized construct grounded in validated innovation-supportive HR practices, including autonomy, developmental feedback, innovation climate and collaboration. It empirically demonstrates the differential dependence of each leadership style on HR systems – revealing TL’s HR-independence and IOL’s HR-dependence. By bridging leadership theory, social exchange theory, dynamic capabilities and strategic human resource management, this research advances the understanding of structural and psychological enablers of innovation. The study offers a configurational perspective linking leadership intent to HR infrastructure, yielding practical strategies for tailoring leadership-HR alignment across organizational and sectoral contexts.

citation_contexts: The study population is a group of professionals in software development industry in Pakistan who include project managers, team members and stakeholders in project-based work.

The majority of research has been done in developed economies and conventional industries like construction and manufacturing and has not paid much attention to the software development sector in the developing countries like Pakistan (Fu & Tan, 2025).
The research was also restricted to the Pakistani software development industry and that can limit the extrapolation of the results to other sectors or nations.

This paper will fill these gaps by investigating the integrated impacts of innovation and Project Control on the sustainable success of projects, where project governance serves as an intermediate variable in the presence of software development projects in Pakistan.

The study contributes to the project management literature by empirically confirming the existence of such relationships within the software development industry in Pakistan, a setting that has received insufficient empirical evidence.

Nevertheless, there is limited empirical data on the mediation role of governance regarding the relationship between innovation, control and sustainable project success especially in the context of software development projects in Pakistan.

This section outlines the statistical analysis and diagnostic steps to be followed to investigate the impact of innovation and Project Control on sustainable project success where project governance is indicated as the mediating mechanism in software development organisations in Pakistan.

results.title: Digital Product Innovation Management: Balancing Stability and Fluidity through Formalization
results.citation: R. Pesch et al. (2021) Digital Product Innovation Management: Balancing Stability and Fluidity through Formalization doi:10.1111/jpim.12609
results.venue: The Journal of product innovation management
results.abstract: While an increasing body of research is considering the role of digital product innovation for firms’ survival, there is still a dearth of studies on how firms can best pursue digital product innovations. The development of these innovations is often accompanied by uncertainty and ambiguity. Thus, firms might benefit from the greater clarity and guidance that result from formalization. However, the application of formalized procedures and structures might also lead to the suppression of fluidity, creativity, or change. Therefore, the purpose of this study is to determine if formalization at the project level can enhance digital product innovation. A partial duality logic integrating evidence of both the positive and negative effects of formalization builds the fundament for the hypothesized model. We use survey and patent data for a sample of 395 firms operating in the manufacturing industry to test the model. The results suggest the diminishing marginal utility of formalization— that is, formalization enhances digital product innovation radicalness and digital product innovation performance but with decreasing positive marginal returns. These findings challenge the widespread assumptions on the relationship between formalization and innovation. This new perspective on the relationship between formalization and innovation helps identify conditions in which formalization can facilitate firms’ efforts to develop profitable, innovative digital products. For example, the results demonstrate that older firms in particular can benefit from formalization. In turn, beyond providing insights for executives, this study offers answers to persistent research questions concerning which organizational arrangements are optimal for enhancing digital product innovation.

results.title: NTIRE 2025 Challenge on Real-World Face Restoration: Methods and Results
results.citation: Zheng Chen et al. (2025) NTIRE 2025 Challenge on Real-World Face Restoration: Methods and Results doi:10.1109/CVPRW67362.2025.00142
results.venue: 2025 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Workshops (CVPRW)
results.abstract: This paper provides a review of the NTIRE 2025 challenge on real-world face restoration, highlighting the proposed solutions and the resulting outcomes. The challenge focuses on generating natural, realistic outputs while maintaining identity consistency. Its goal is to advance state-of-the-art solutions for perceptual quality and realism, without imposing constraints on computational resources or training data. The track of the challenge evaluates performance using a weighted image quality assessment (IQA) score and employs the AdaFace model as an identity checker. The competition attracted 141 registrants, with 13 teams submitting valid models, and ultimately, 10 teams achieved a valid score in the final ranking. This collaborative effort advances the performance of real-world face restoration while offering an in-depth overview of the latest trends in the field.

results.title: Strategic alignment of organizational structure based on decisions for sustainable organizational performance: a bibliometric-systematic literature review
results.citation: M. Stanikzai et al. (2025) Strategic alignment of organizational structure based on decisions for sustainable organizational performance: a bibliometric-systematic literature review doi:10.1080/23311975.2025.2560650
results.abstract: Abstract This bibliometric-systematic literature review (BSLR) examines the strategic alignment of organizational structure based on decisions (OSbDs), focusing on centralized, decentralized, and AI-driven decisions across the executive, managerial, and operational levels to achieve sustainable organizational performance (SOP). While traditional organizational structures are well-documented, OSbDs remain underexplored. We systematically reviewed literature from 2014 to 2025 across three academic databases (Scopus, Dimension AI, and Google Scholar), analyzing 112 documents for relevance and quality. The findings generated four thematic clusters visualized through VOSviewer Term and Keyword Co-Occurrence Network Analyses and a conceptual framework. The study adheres to PRISMA 2020 guidelines, ensuring transparency. The BSLR provides a comprehensive overview of existing scientific research. The largest cluster (34.15% of 82 documents) focuses on AI and automation, reflecting a research emphasis on operational efficiency, which underscores the impact on SOP. We also outlined a table of future research directions. The findings propose a hybrid decision-making framework that integrates centralized, decentralized, and automated/AI models with strategic alignment. This supports SOP by fostering strategic consistency across organizational levels, offering insights for academics and implications for leaders adapting OSbDs to enhance effectiveness, efficiency, and innovation in their organizations.

results.title: Gauging the Gap: Student Perceptions of Skill Proficiency in Skill-Based Education within Schools of Punjab, Pakistan
results.citation: Muhammad Rafiq-uz-Zaman et al. (2025) Gauging the Gap: Student Perceptions of Skill Proficiency in Skill-Based Education within Schools of Punjab, Pakistan doi:10.63056/acad.004.03.0553
results.venue: ACADEMIA International Journal for Social Sciences
results.abstract: This study investigates student-perceived proficiency in soft and practical skills acquired through skill-based education (SBE) in schools. A quantitative approach was employed, utilizing a structured questionnaire administered to 384 twelfth grade students (158 males and 226 females) across 50% public higher secondary schools of three other than divisional headquarters cities of Punjab province, namely Jehlum, Chiniot, and Bahawalnagar. The survey assessed students’ self-reported proficiency in 20 soft skills (e.g., data analysis, communication, critical thinking) and 20 practical skills (e.g., welding, IT skills, cooking), using a five-point Likert scale. Data analysis, involving frequency distributions, weighted scores, mean values, standard deviations, and rank ordering, revealed significant disparities in perceived proficiency. Students reported the lowest levels of proficiency in foundational areas such as data analysis, research skills and critical thinking skills among soft skills, and welding and woodwork among practical skills. While skills such as cultural sensitivity, self-awareness, and sports were rated higher, most skills were only perceived as moderately proficient. From all over soft and practical skills, mostly were slightly proficient.  These findings underscore a significant gap between the intended outcomes of SBE and students’ perceived skill acquisition. The study highlights the need for a reevaluation and refinement of SBE implementation strategies. This research contributes valuable student-centered insights for educators and policymakers seeking to enhance SBE programs in schools. The study's limitations include its reliance on self-reported data, cross-sectional design and limited geographic scope.

results.title: Digital Transformation in Project Management: A Systematic Review and Research Agenda
results.citation: Meiying Chen et al. (2025) Digital Transformation in Project Management: A Systematic Review and Research Agenda doi:10.3390/systems13080625
results.abstract: Digital transformation (DT) fundamentally reshapes how organisations operate, create value, and respond to complex environments through the integration of digital technologies. Beyond mere technical deployment, DT involves behavioural, strategic, and institutional changes. As these transformations are increasingly managed through projects and programmes, project management plays a pivotal role, not only in delivering these transformations but also in enabling them. However, the relationship between DT and project management remains fragmented and insufficiently explored. This paper addresses this gap by systematically reviewing 66 peer-reviewed articles using a qualitative thematic coding approach informed by sociotechnical systems theory. The analysis reveals four interrelated themes: methodologies and the sociotechnical integration of digital tools, misalignments in sociotechnical interfaces, governance and leadership, and industry- or project-specific transformation trajectories. Based on these findings, the paper proposes three key future research agenda: (1) embedding digital tools through methodological mediation and governance integration, (2) governance and leadership as strategic enablers, and (3) advancing sector-specific insights into DT. By offering a structured synthesis and a theory-driven research agenda, this review contributes to a more integrated understanding of how DT unfolds within project-based contexts and lays the groundwork for future interdisciplinary research.

results.title: Between Adoption and Ambiguity: Navigating the AI Policy Vacuum in Pakistani Higher Education
results.citation: Muhammad Rafiq-uz-Zaman (2025) Between Adoption and Ambiguity: Navigating the AI Policy Vacuum in Pakistani Higher Education doi:10.71317/rjsa.003.06.0523
results.abstract: Large language models, grammar assistants, automated analytics, and other artificial intelligence (AI) tools have quickly changed higher education around the globe. However, in Pakistan, their application in an institutional setting has not been tightly regulated. This critical analysis explores the widening divide between individual implementation and official government regulation of AI in Pakistani institutions of higher learning, by referencing the Islamia University of Bahawalpur (IUB) as an allegorical example of policy laggardness in the face of excitement. The article presents arguments based on recent international and open-access literature, such as UNESCO (2023), Jisc (2024), as well as 2024-2025 empirical studies that argue that AI avoidance or silence is worse than directed integration. Even though some universities have developed explicit ethical guidelines on the use of AI, including Oxford, Monash, Stanford, and the National University of Singapore, Higher Education Commission of Pakistan and large universities still use old policies on plagiarism. The paper supports specific changes: the development of a national algorithm of ethical use of AI in the educational process, the creation of university-level policy units in the field of AI, training of faculty employees in pedagogy, reforming the quality assurance standards, and the mandatory publicity of AI employment in academic activities. It presents a case that Pakistan must shift towards rhetorical adoption to responsible integration. As long as organizations such as IUB connect their AI ambitions to transparent governance, Pakistan can be a regional leader of ethical and inclusive AI use in higher education in the Global South.

results.title: Interplay between leadership style and innovative work behavior: mediated by innovation-oriented people management
results.citation: Qiang Fu et al. (2025) Interplay between leadership style and innovative work behavior: mediated by innovation-oriented people management doi:10.1108/ijis-03-2025-0104
results.abstract:
This study aims to investigate how transformational leadership (TL) and innovation-oriented leadership (IOL) influence innovative work behavior (IWB), with innovation-oriented people management (IOPM) serving as a mediating mechanism. It offers a novel dual-path framework to understand the structural and psychological dynamics through which leadership drives innovation.
 
 
 
 A quantitative cross-sectional survey was conducted among 158 employees across innovation-active organizations in Singapore, including sectors such as ICT, manufacturing, construction and retail. Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) and Process Macro (Model 4) with bootstrapping were employed to analyze both direct and indirect effects.
 
 
 
 The dual-path model offers superior predictive power, because both TL and IOL significantly enhance IWB, but through distinct mechanisms. TL operates more autonomously via intrinsic motivation, whereas IOL relies heavily on structured human resource (HR) systems (IOPM). IOPM partially mediates the relationship between both leadership styles and IWB, with a stronger mediating effect observed in the IOL–IWB pathway (51.94%) compared to TL–IWB (26.42%).
 
 
 
 The study is limited by its cross-sectional design, reliance on self-reported data and geographic focus on Singapore. Future research should apply longitudinal, multi-source designs and include additional leadership styles and control variables to improve generalizability.
 
 
 
 Organizations should align leadership strategies with their structural contexts. TL may be more effective in hierarchical or resource-constrained settings, whereas IOL requires supportive HR infrastructures. Policymakers and HR leaders can use these insights to tailor leadership development and innovation-support systems by sector.
 
 
 
 This study highlights the critical role of leadership–HR alignment in shaping workplace innovation and employee empowerment. By clarifying how transformational and innovation-oriented leadership styles foster innovative work behavior, it contributes to building inclusive, participatory and psychologically safe work environments. The findings support broader social objectives of employee well-being, equity in idea contribution and sustainable organizational learning. Moreover, the emphasis on innovation-oriented people management systems offers pathways to enhance workforce adaptability and engagement in the digital age. These insights can inform social policies aimed at fostering human capital development and inclusive innovation across sectors and demographic groups.
 
 
 
 This study introduces an integrated dual-pathway model that distinguishes how transformational and innovation-oriented leadership uniquely influence IWB through IOPM. IOPM is conceptualized as a synthesized construct grounded in validated innovation-supportive HR practices, including autonomy, developmental feedback, innovation climate and collaboration. It empirically demonstrates the differential dependence of each leadership style on HR systems – revealing TL’s HR-independence and IOL’s HR-dependence. By bridging leadership theory, social exchange theory, dynamic capabilities and strategic human resource management, this research advances the understanding of structural and psychological enablers of innovation. The study offers a configurational perspective linking leadership intent to HR infrastructure, yielding practical strategies for tailoring leadership-HR alignment across organizational and sectoral contexts.

citation_contexts: The study population is a group of professionals in software development industry in Pakistan who include project managers, team members and stakeholders in project-based work.

The majority of research has been done in developed economies and conventional industries like construction and manufacturing and has not paid much attention to the software development sector in the developing countries like Pakistan (Fu & Tan, 2025).
The research was also restricted to the Pakistani software development industry and that can limit the extrapolation of the results to other sectors or nations.

This paper will fill these gaps by investigating the integrated impacts of innovation and Project Control on the sustainable success of projects, where project governance serves as an intermediate variable in the presence of software development projects in Pakistan.

The study contributes to the project management literature by empirically confirming the existence of such relationships within the software development industry in Pakistan, a setting that has received insufficient empirical evidence.

Nevertheless, there is limited empirical data on the mediation role of governance regarding the relationship between innovation, control and sustainable project success especially in the context of software development projects in Pakistan.

This section outlines the statistical analysis and diagnostic steps to be followed to investigate the impact of innovation and Project Control on sustainable project success where project governance is indicated as the mediating mechanism in software development organisations in Pakistan.

results.title: Digital Product Innovation Management: Balancing Stability and Fluidity through Formalization
results.citation: R. Pesch et al. (2021) Digital Product Innovation Management: Balancing Stability and Fluidity through Formalization doi:10.1111/jpim.12609
results.venue: The Journal of product innovation management
results.abstract: While an increasing body of research is considering the role of digital product innovation for firms’ survival, there is still a dearth of studies on how firms can best pursue digital product innovations. The development of these innovations is often accompanied by uncertainty and ambiguity. Thus, firms might benefit from the greater clarity and guidance that result from formalization. However, the application of formalized procedures and structures might also lead to the suppression of fluidity, creativity, or change. Therefore, the purpose of this study is to determine if formalization at the project level can enhance digital product innovation. A partial duality logic integrating evidence of both the positive and negative effects of formalization builds the fundament for the hypothesized model. We use survey and patent data for a sample of 395 firms operating in the manufacturing industry to test the model. The results suggest the diminishing marginal utility of formalization— that is, formalization enhances digital product innovation radicalness and digital product innovation performance but with decreasing positive marginal returns. These findings challenge the widespread assumptions on the relationship between formalization and innovation. This new perspective on the relationship between formalization and innovation helps identify conditions in which formalization can facilitate firms’ efforts to develop profitable, innovative digital products. For example, the results demonstrate that older firms in particular can benefit from formalization. In turn, beyond providing insights for executives, this study offers answers to persistent research questions concerning which organizational arrangements are optimal for enhancing digital product innovation.

results.title: NTIRE 2025 Challenge on Real-World Face Restoration: Methods and Results
results.citation: Zheng Chen et al. (2025) NTIRE 2025 Challenge on Real-World Face Restoration: Methods and Results doi:10.1109/CVPRW67362.2025.00142
results.venue: 2025 IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition Workshops (CVPRW)
results.abstract: This paper provides a review of the NTIRE 2025 challenge on real-world face restoration, highlighting the proposed solutions and the resulting outcomes. The challenge focuses on generating natural, realistic outputs while maintaining identity consistency. Its goal is to advance state-of-the-art solutions for perceptual quality and realism, without imposing constraints on computational resources or training data. The track of the challenge evaluates performance using a weighted image quality assessment (IQA) score and employs the AdaFace model as an identity checker. The competition attracted 141 registrants, with 13 teams submitting valid models, and ultimately, 10 teams achieved a valid score in the final ranking. This collaborative effort advances the performance of real-world face restoration while offering an in-depth overview of the latest trends in the field.

results.title: Strategic alignment of organizational structure based on decisions for sustainable organizational performance: a bibliometric-systematic literature review
results.citation: M. Stanikzai et al. (2025) Strategic alignment of organizational structure based on decisions for sustainable organizational performance: a bibliometric-systematic literature review doi:10.1080/23311975.2025.2560650
results.abstract: Abstract This bibliometric-systematic literature review (BSLR) examines the strategic alignment of organizational structure based on decisions (OSbDs), focusing on centralized, decentralized, and AI-driven decisions across the executive, managerial, and operational levels to achieve sustainable organizational performance (SOP). While traditional organizational structures are well-documented, OSbDs remain underexplored. We systematically reviewed literature from 2014 to 2025 across three academic databases (Scopus, Dimension AI, and Google Scholar), analyzing 112 documents for relevance and quality. The findings generated four thematic clusters visualized through VOSviewer Term and Keyword Co-Occurrence Network Analyses and a conceptual framework. The study adheres to PRISMA 2020 guidelines, ensuring transparency. The BSLR provides a comprehensive overview of existing scientific research. The largest cluster (34.15% of 82 documents) focuses on AI and automation, reflecting a research emphasis on operational efficiency, which underscores the impact on SOP. We also outlined a table of future research directions. The findings propose a hybrid decision-making framework that integrates centralized, decentralized, and automated/AI models with strategic alignment. This supports SOP by fostering strategic consistency across organizational levels, offering insights for academics and implications for leaders adapting OSbDs to enhance effectiveness, efficiency, and innovation in their organizations.
